hypothesis: A multimodal cross-modal predictive model that jointly trains graph, text, and XRD representations using cross-modal prediction losses, VIC regularization, and contrastive loss will achieve lower mean absolute error on crystal property prediction tasks (e.g., band gap, shear modulus, bulk modulus, dielectric, perovskite formation energy) than state-of-the-art graph-only baselines (CGCNN, CoGN, ALIGNN) and will generalize to large external datasets (e.g., AFLOW) even when only ~0.6% of model parameters are updated during finetuning.
